ref: Joseph's brothers killed a goat and dipped Joseph's fancy coat in its blood.
text: Ka ba n…Ēk Josef fuug la, ka k č b č čgi n…Ēk fuug la mi'is ziimin la.
translated: They gave Joseph the food, and he ate the food until it was finished.

ref: After this, they took the coat to their father and said, ‚ÄúWe found this! Look at it carefully and see if it belongs to your son.‚ÄĚ
text: Ba da m…Ēr fuv…õnliŇč la l…õbi ba saam san'an y…õl o ye, ‚ÄúTi pi'ig n…õ'…õŇča n…õ, di'em g…Ēs ye li an…õ f č biig la fuug…Ē…Ē?‚ÄĚ
translated: They had a discussion and returned to their father to ask him, saying, ‚ÄúWe are completely fed up, how can it be that your child and your children...?

ref: About that time Judah left his brothers in the hill country and went to live near his friend Hirah in the town of Adullam.
text: SaŇčkan la Juda da bas o ba'abiis ka keŇči b…õ dau s…Ē' san'an Adullam teŇčin, ka o y č' čr buon Hira.
translated: Then Judah left his brothers and went to a certain Adullamite whose name was Hira.

ref: Tamar found out that her father-in-law Judah was going to Timnah to shear his sheep.
text: Ba da y…õl Tamar ye, ‚ÄúF č dayaam dau la b…õ suori wa'ae Timna ye o kies o p…õ'…õs k…Ēnbid la.‚ÄĚ
translated: They said to Tamar, "Your father-in-law is going up to Timna to shear his sheep.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, ‚ÄúWhere is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?‚ÄĚ ‚ÄúThere's never been one here,‚ÄĚ they answered.
text: Ka o bu'os dap ban…õ b…õ…õ anina la ye, ‚ÄúTeŇčgban pu'ag…Ē…Ēndir kan…õ zin' suor kpikp…õŇč Enaim teŇč za'an…Ē…Ērin la b…õ ya?‚ÄĚ Ka ba y…õl o ye, ‚ÄúTeŇčgban pu'ag…Ē…Ēndir ka' kp…õlaa.‚ÄĚ
translated: And he asked his companions and the people, saying, ‚ÄúIs it the chief of the land who is sitting at the great gate of the city of Enam?‚ÄĚ And they told him, saying, ‚ÄúIt is the chief of the land who has arrived.

ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, ‚ÄúI couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka o l…õb Juda san'an y…õl o ye, ‚ÄúMam p č ny…õ…õ o, din…õ l…õm paas la dap ban…õ b…õ…õ anina la ye, ‚ÄėTeŇčgban pu'ag…Ē…Ēndir ka' kp…õlaa.‚Äô‚ÄĚ
translated: Then he turned to Judah and said to him, "I cannot find him until we cross the land that they call 'TeŇčgban pu'ag…Ē…Ēndir ka' kp…õla

ref: ‚ÄúIf you couldn't find her, we'll just let her keep the things I gave her,‚ÄĚ Judah answered. ‚ÄúAnd we'd better forget about the goat, or else we'll look like fools.‚ÄĚ
text: Juda da y…õl ye, ‚ÄúOn m…Ēr si'el la k…õl ka o m…Ēri keŇč; ya'a ka' ala, ba na la'adi ti. Li ya'a an sida, m p č pan'as o, b…Ēzug…Ē m t čm ya ye ba m…Ēr b čp…Ēl la keŇči tis o ka p č ny…õ…õ o.‚ÄĚ
translated: Judah said, "If he has a wife and he has children; if he says that, they will believe us. If it is not so, I will not punish him, because I sent you to take the children to him and not to harm him.

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, ‚ÄúThe man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.‚ÄĚ
text: Ban m…Ēr Tamar n yina la, o t čm labaari y…õl o dayaam dau la ye, ‚ÄúM m…Ēr n…õ p č čg ka li an…õ dau kan…õ s č'oe la'abama nwa' tisim.‚ÄĚ Ka y…õl ye, ‚ÄúG…Ēsim ye f č na baŇč la'abama an s…Ē' b čnn…õ…õ? Li an…õ baŇč n…õ li nwiig, n…õ dansaar.‚ÄĚ
translated: When Tamar's brother came out, he sent a message to his father saying, "I have a plan to make that man's work succeed." And he said, "How will you know if the work is good? You will know it by its fruit, and its dance.

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: T…Ē', ka ba da m…Ēr Josef n keŇč Egipt ka Potifa on…õ an Faaro na'ayikp…õ…õm ka an ban…õ gur Na'ab la tuongat la da' o Isimael dim ban…õ m…Ēr o na la san'an.
translated: So, when they had sold Joseph and he went to Egypt, Potiphar, one of Pharaoh's officials, bought him from the Ishmaelites who had brought him there.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: o zugdaan la da ny…õ ka Zugs…Ēb b…õ n…õ o, ka lin…õ ka o t čm w čsa da kenn…õ tuon s č' čŇča.
translated: His leadership was such that God was with him, and because of that, all his work was successful.

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: La'an n…õ on da zan'as la, pu'a la da sin'igid Josef n…õ daar w čsa, ka o m…õ da zan'as ye o k č digin n…õ o b…õ…õ li'el o san'an…õ.
translated: When he was born, his father and mother named him Joseph, so that he would be born to be a helper and a comfort to his people.

ref: And when he heard me scream, he ran out of the house, leaving his coat with me.‚ÄĚ
text: ka on w čm ka m kaas ye ba faanmim la, ka o z…Ē ka bas o fuug č m nu'usin.‚ÄĚ
translated: And he heard that I said they were mocking him, so he ran and left his bag in my hand.

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: ka o m…Ēr Josef n keŇč y…Ē' zin'ikan…õ ka ba y…Ē…Ēd na'ab nimban…õ da t čm b…õ'…õd la. Josef n da b…õ sanrega ni la,
translated: He saw Josef going to the market where they sell animal skins and do work. Josef was with his friends.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: O da n…Ēki ba y…Ē' on…õ an ban…õ gur na'ab la tuongat yin, d…Ēkan…õ ka ba da y…Ē' Josef ani la m…õn.
translated: He will tell them, you and your brothers, who sold Joseph, just as they sold Joseph and him.

ref: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
text: Ban…õ gur na'ab la tuongat da n…Ēki ba niŇč Josef nu'usin ka o g…Ēsidi ba. Ba da b…õ…õ anina ka li yuug bi'ela.
translated: The next morning they went to look for Josef to ask him to guide them. They were afraid that they would get lost in the wilderness.

ref: ‚ÄúWe each had a dream last night,‚ÄĚ they answered, ‚Äúand there is no one to tell us what they mean.‚ÄĚ Joseph replied, ‚ÄúDoesn't God know the meaning of dreams? Now tell me what you dreamed.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka ba l…õbis ye, ‚ÄúTi babayi' nwa' sa zaansimn…õ y č' čŇč, amaa s…Ē' kae na pa'ali ti li gbinn…õ.‚ÄĚ Ka Josef l…õbis ye, ‚ÄúAbaŇč zaans čŇč gbin p č yit n…õ Wina'am ni naa? Y…õliminim…õ ya zaans čŇč la!‚ÄĚ
translated: And they answered, ‚ÄúOur father's dream is a deep dream, but there is no one who can interpret it for us.‚ÄĚ And Joseph answered, ‚ÄúDoes not the interpretation of dreams belong to God? Tell me your dreams!

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Daba atan' nwa' s č čgin, Faaro na yisif kp…õla ka k…õ ka f č l…õbi t čmmi f č t č čma w č č f čn daa p čn t čm si'em la, ka f č na n…Ēk Faaro nwammi niŇč o nu'usin w č č f čn daa kuobid o daam si'em la.
translated: When the sun rises in the morning, Pharaoh and Joseph will come so that you may return to your work which you used to do before, and you will tend Pharaoh's livestock which you used to herd before.

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: Ba mukim n…õ ziim…õ yi Hibiru teŇčin na, hali kp…õla nwa' m p č t čm si'el din…õ nar ye m b…õ b čmb…Ēk kaŇča nii.‚ÄĚ
translated: They lived and died in the land of the Hebrews, until the time came for me to do something else, and I became a wanderer.

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.‚ÄĚ
text: ka p…õ'…õkan…õ b…õ zugin la, ka bodobodo buudi w čsa lin…õ an Faaro din b…õ…õ di ni, amaa ka niis …Ēnbid bodobodo la.‚ÄĚ
translated: And the magicians did likewise, and frogs came up on Pharaoh's land, but they could not remove the frogs.

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: ka yul on…õ aan o bodobodo maannib kp…õ…õm la nw…õn…õ Josef n da pa'ali ba zaans čŇč la gbin si'em la.
translated: And he told them that his many brothers and Joseph had done evil to them in the past.
